title: Twenty Minutes of Cycling Rewires the Hippocampus in Real Time
description: For the first time, scientists used implanted electrodes in living humans to watch a single bout of exercise change the brain in real time. Twenty minutes of light cycling was enough to increase hippocampal sharp wave-ripples — the brain's internal memory replay mechanism — and strengthen connectivity across memory networks. The catch: the subjects had epilepsy, and the implications for healthy brains remain genuinely open. Here's what the data actually shows.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Your Brain Starts Rewiring for Memory Twenty Minutes Into a Bike Ride. Scientists Just Watched It Happen.
